dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7721 IH: Combating Regulatory Abuse, Closing Known Deficiencies, and Overseeing Waste Nationwide Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Grothman introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Education and Workforce

form.official-title: To amend the Child Care and Development Block Grant Act of 1990 to implement an improper payment threshold under such Act.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Combating Regulatory Abuse, Closing Known Deficiencies, and Overseeing Waste Nationwide Act or the CRACKDOWN Act of 2026.

section.header: Overpayment rate requiring corrective action plan; conditional ineligibility
section.text: Section 658J of the Child Care and Development Block Grant Act of 1990 (42 U.S.C. 9858h) is amended—

paragraph.text: by redesignating subsection (c) as subsection (e), and
by inserting after subsection (b) the following:

subsection.header: Overpayment threshold requiring corrective action plan
subsection.text: If for a fiscal year the overpayment rate of a State is more than 5 percent of the aggregate amount of payments made to carry out this subchapter by such State for such fiscal year, then such State shall submit to the Secretary—

paragraph.text: for review and approval a corrective action plan to reduce such rate to not more than 5 percent for each subsequent fiscal year; and
such reports as the Secretary may require to show that such State is complying with the requirements of such plan as approved by the Secretary.

subsection.text: If for each of 2 consecutive fiscal years the overpayment rate of a State determined under this section is more 5 percent, then such State shall be ineligible to receive funds under this subchapter unless such State demonstrates to the satisfaction of the Secretary that such State for the next fiscal year will—

paragraph.text: reduce such overpayment rate to not more than 5 percent for the next fiscal year; or
make significant progress to comply with the corrective action plan approved under subsection (c).
